news.title: CRO Fortrea launches AI-powered solution to improve trial efficiency
news.snippet: Fortrea is expanding its capabilities with a suite of AI-powered technologies designed to automate workflows, streamline oversight and improve productivity for trial sponsors.

news.title: E2 lands $80M series C for next-gen thrombectomy treatment
news.snippet: California-based medtech E2 has raised $80 million in a series C funding round as it looks to commercialize its pulmonary embolism treatment.

news.title: Owlet CEO exits suddenly, co-founder returns to the nest
news.snippet: Owlet is quickly rearranging its nest as Jonathan Harris has resigned, effective immediately, as president and CEO of the pediatric device company.

news.title: Longtime Hologic CEO Steve MacMillan to retire as $18.3B deal closes
news.snippet: Hologic President and CEO Steve MacMillan is stepping down after nearly 13 years, marking the end of an era for the medtech company.

news.title: Lilly pays AC Immune $12.5M to expand Alzheimer’s collab as asset draws closer to clinic
news.snippet: Eli Lilly has handed AC Immune 10 million Swiss francs ($12.5 million) to expand their Alzheimer’s disease collaboration as a candidate moves closer to the clinic.

news.title: Gilead pays $3.1B upfront for Tubulis to bolster oncology ADC pipeline
news.snippet: Gilead has struck a deal to buy Tubulis for $3.15 billion upfront and up to $1.85 billion in milestones, securing an antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) candidate that is racing toward pivotal trials.

news.title: Biogen links up with Alloy platform to boost ASO drug development
news.snippet: The agreement allows Biogen to apply the platform to advance antisense therapeutics against multiple undisclosed targets. Alloy will receive an upfront payment and is eligible for milestone payments a

news.title: Sanofi’s bispecific scores double respiratory phase 2 wins, but flunks eczema study
news.snippet: Sanofi’s TSLP and IL-13 inhibitor has aced a pair of mid-stage studies for respiratory diseases, but missed on a phase 2 eczema trial.

news.title: AI model designs new treatment candidate for opioid addiction that cuts cravings in rats
news.snippet: While the opioid epidemic is one of the most urgent public health emergencies facing the U.S., new therapeutic approaches for treating the underlying substance use disorder have lagged far behind. Sci

news.title: Takeda tears up Denali partnership, returning dementia asset amid restructuring
news.snippet: Takeda has terminated a neuro partnership with Denali Therapeutics as the Japanese pharma undergoes a massive restructuring marked by significant layoffs.

news.title: Anthropic acquires stealth AI startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: reports
news.snippet: AI powerhouse Anthropic is continuing its push into the healthcare arena with the acquisition of previously stealth AI startup Coefficient Bio in a $400 million stock deal, according to reporting from

news.title: Zeto’s latest EEG device garners FDA 510(k) clearance for outpatient use
news.snippet: Zeto, which makes brainwave monitors for emergency rooms, received a third FDA clearance for an EEG device. The latest is intended for use in outpatient clinics or at home.

news.title: US, UK regulators bolster medical device collaboration while lifting tariffs
news.snippet: The U.S. FDA and the U.K.’s Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency agreed to bolster their ongoing medical technology collaboration while also removing tariffs on British medicine exports

news.title: With Sanofi and Pfizer deals, Novavax bets on ‘amplification strategy’ to drive vaccines engine
news.snippet: After shedding expensive commercial infrastructure and pivoting to a partnership model, the company is now positioning its Matrix-M vaccine adjuvant as a versatile, license-ready asset for other bioph

news.title: How ctDNA is redefining MRD monitoring in hematologic cancers
news.snippet: ctDNA assays are changing how hematologic cancers are monitored, from MRD detection to clinical trial design.

news.title: With Gardasil demand shortfall in China, Merck and Zhifei adjust their partnership
news.snippet: The plummeting demand for Merck’s HPV vaccine Gardasil in China has forced the New Jersey drugmaker to rework its arrangement with its partner in the country, Zhifei Biological Products.

news.title: Vertex taps Halozyme and its recently acquired Elektrofi tech in $15M drug delivery deal
news.snippet: Halozyme is putting a recent drug-delivery acquisition to work thanks to a new tie-up with Vertex Pharmaceuticals.

news.title: FDA seeks legislative teeth to bite back against misleading DTC drug ads
news.snippet: The FDA is seeking new powers to hold companies accountable for misleading direct-to-consumer ads, adding legislative weight to its pushback against deceptive drug advertising.

news.title: Ardena’s US plant buy pays off as CDMO endures rough geopolitical waters
news.snippet: “You want to be holistic, but within a particular specialty,” Ardena CEO Jeremie Trochu recently told Fierce about how the CDMO is navigating rough geopolitical waters.

news.title: ImmunityBio responds to FDA scrutiny over Anktiva promotional claims with new protocols
news.snippet: In response to a warning letter from the FDA that accused the company of making “false or misleading” claims about its bladder cancer drug Anktiva, ImmunityBio says it’s implementing enhanced protocol

news.title: Insulet's Omnipod takes Type 2 diabetes representation to the TV screen in 'Scrubs' revival
news.snippet: A recent episode of ABC's Scrubs featured an on-screen brand integration with Insulet's Omnipod device worn by beloved TV doctor Christopher Turk.

news.title: Neurocrine, eying ‘blockbuster in the making,’ strikes its largest-ever M&A deal with $2.9B Soleno buyout
news.snippet: Neurocrine Biosciences, forever a possible M&A target in the biopharma industry, is making a major acquisition itself. The purchase adds to a recent string of multibillion-dollar deals.

news.title: Amgen scores with trial of on-body injected version of Tepezza in thyroid eye disease
news.snippet: With a new, patient-friendly route of administration, Amgen may have found a way to increase demand for Tepezza. A phase 3 trial of patients with thyroid eye disease has shown that subcutaneous inject

news.title: #FierceMadness: The championship round is here—VOTE NOW
news.snippet: It’s time once again for Fierce Pharma Marketing’s annual #FierceMadness tournament. This time around, we're watching 64 newly minted drug names go head to head.

news.title: VML Health urges marketers to shift from lifespan to ‘joyspan’ as patient goals evolve
news.snippet: VML Health has published a report about 10 trends defining the future of healthcare, making the case that pharma marketers must adapt to patients shifting their goals from lifespan to “joyspan.”

news.title: Eli Lilly takes the court with 150th anniversary campaign to catch Final Four crowd
news.snippet: Eli Lilly has published a video about how medicines affect lives to mark its 150th anniversary and the arrival of college basketball stars in its home city.

news.title: With Sanofi and Pfizer deals, Novavax bets on ‘amplification strategy’ to drive vaccines engine
news.snippet: After shedding expensive commercial infrastructure and pivoting to a partnership model, the company is now positioning its Matrix-M vaccine adjuvant as a versatile, license-ready asset for other bioph

news.title: AI in medical communications: Where expertise still matters
news.snippet: AI can speed content creation, but human judgment drives credibility, context and behavior change in medical communications.

news.title: March M&A surge triggers high expectations for 2026
news.snippet: In a flurry of activity, biopharma companies pulled off seven transactions in the last two weeks of March worth a combined $29 billion. The blitz indicates that 2026 will be a prime year for M&A a

news.title: An update on the pharma industry’s reshoring effort
news.snippet: Where does pharma’s reshoring push stand today, and what comes next? Reshoring Institute Executive Director Rosemary Coates explains the industry’s unique advantage and the hurdles still in the way.

news.title: Judge refuses to block sending abortion pill by mail for now, but says FDA must finish review
news.snippet: A federal judge Tuesday refused to block filling prescriptions for the abortion pill mifepristone by mail across the U.S. — at least for now.

news.title: STAT+: Novo Nordisk launches high-dose Wegovy
news.snippet: Insurers score Medicare Advantage wins, the obesity drug race continues, and other biotech news from The Readout

news.title: STAT+: Trump budget’s ‘America First’ drug policy proposals
news.snippet: Buried in Trump's 2027 budget proposal are some new ideas to bring drug manufacturing to the U.S.

news.title: STAT+: Many cancer patients don’t get genomic tests to guide treatment, study finds
news.snippet: Even as therapies improve, a startling number of cancer patients are not getting genomic tests that could improve their chance of survival.

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about FDA backing domestic production, another Gilead deal, and more
news.snippet: The FDA used the president’s budget to propose policies aimed at encouraging domestic development and manufacturing of drugs

news.title: If you cap insulin at $35 a month, people with type 2 diabetes stick to treatment, study finds
news.snippet: $35 insulin, more evidence abortion meds are safe OTC, and other health news from Morning Rounds

news.title: STAT+: Gilead to buy cancer biotech Tubulis for more than $3 billion
news.snippet: Gilead is buying the German biotech Tubulis for more than $3 billion, a move that will expand its oncology pipeline.

news.title: STAT+: Merck’s experimental HIV prevention pill could be made for less than $5 a year, researchers say
news.snippet: Resarchers say Merck should 'urgently begin licensing generic production to its HIV prevention pill.

news.title: STAT+: FDA backs proposals to entice pharma companies to test, make drugs domestically
news.snippet: From tariffs to incentives, the administration wants to push companies to manufacture drugs in the U.S.

news.title: Prince William praises £20m milestone for Bowelbabe fund
news.snippet: The Bowelbabe fund, set up by Dame Deborah James in 2022, helps to support Cancer Research UK.

news.title: Weight-loss jabs will be offered on NHS for people at risk of further heart attacks
news.snippet: More than a million people in England will start being offered the anti-obesity jab for better heart health and to avoid strokes.

news.title: 'Something wasn't right': Wrong sperm given to UK families by IVF clinics in northern Cyprus
news.snippet: Families of seven children believe the wrong sperm or egg donors were used in their IVF treatment.
